zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de Massey of Tatton於1225年出生在英國爾鄧翰 梅西時，他的父親Hamon de Massey V30歲，母親Alice de Whitney19歲。他於大約1253年與Margery Mainwaring結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1328年在英國英格蘭爾鄧翰 梅西享年103歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William de Massey of Tatton于1225年出生在英国爾鄧翰 梅西时，他的父亲Hamon de Massey V30岁，母亲Alice de Whitney19岁。他于大约1253年与Margery Mainwaring结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1328年在英国英格兰爾鄧翰 梅西享年103岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Masey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Massey of Tatton was born in 1225, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, his father, Hamon de Massey V, was 30 and his mother, Alice de Whitney, was 19. He married Margery Mainwaring about 1253. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1328, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 103.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de Massey of Tatton est né en 1225, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Hamon de Massey V, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Alice de Whitney, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Margery Mainwaring environ 1253. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1328, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 103 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William de Massey of Tattonが1225年にイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイで生まれた時，父Hamon de Massey Vは30歳，母Alice de Whitneyは19歳であった。約1253年にMargery Mainwaringと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1328年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイで103歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Massey of Tatton(이)가 1225년 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hamon de Massey V(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Whitney(은)는 19세였다. 그는 대략 1253년 Margery Mainwaring(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1328년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 던함 마시에서 103세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de Massey of Tatton родился в 1225 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Hamon de Massey V было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Whitney, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Mainwaring около 1253 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1328 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 103 лет.
